##NAMES=Romeite
##RRUFFID=R060736
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(Ca,Fe^2+^,Mn^2+^,Na)_2_(Sb^5+^,Ti^4+^)_2_O_6_(O,OH,F)
##LOCALITY=Le Cetine di Cotorniano, Chiusdino, Siena Province, Tuscany, Italy
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Brown octahedra associated with brizziite, R060611
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R060736
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=dark core: (Ca_1.06_Na_0.70_Fe^2+^_0.18_Mg_0.06_)_Σ=2_Sb^5+^_2.00_O_6_((OH)_0.70_O_0.30_)_Σ=1_; trace amounts of F; OH estimated by charge balance; rim: (Na_1.10_Sb^3+^_0.64_Fe^3+^_0.10_Mg_0.07_Ca_0.06_Mn_0.02_Pb_0.01_)_Σ=2_Sb^5+^_2.00_O_6_(OH); 
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.303 b: 10.303 c: 10.303 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1093.8 crystal system: cubic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=785
